commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=3fac1426c995ece463dc39a72db44956d7145e3a)
commands.review_summary:
The branch adds core-side Anthropic Claude 4.x image-input normalization to inline provider model input resolution, adds resolver tests, and updates the changelog.

Reproducibility: no. high-confidence user-facing current-main repro remains from the code path I inspected: configured inline matches are normalized through the Anthropic provider hook. The helper-level behavior in the PR body is source-reproducible because current resolveProviderModelInput only repairs Foundry before plugin normalization runs.
commands.review_followup: Needs maintainer or contributor revision because the remaining question is whether a provider-owned seam is needed; automation cannot supply the contributor's required real behavior proof.

target.title: fix(anthropic): preserve configured Claude image capability
target.body:
## Summary
- normalize stale text-only Anthropic/Claude 4 catalog rows to text+image in the core configured model input path
- cover claude-sonnet-4-5 configured/local catalog behavior while preserving text-only older Claude rows
- add changelog entry

## Real behavior proof
Source-level regression for the reported path:
- `resolveProviderModelInput({ provider: "anthropic", modelId: "claude-sonnet-4-5", input: ["text"] })` now returns `["text", "image"]`
- older `claude-3-haiku-20240307` with `input: ["text"]` remains text-only

## Validation
- `pnpm test src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/model.inline-provider.test.ts`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 CHANGELOG.md src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/model.inline-provider.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/model.inline-provider.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`

Follow-up for https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/83756#issuecomment-4487709808.
target.author: turbotheturtle

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
